debug_info.goal:
V : Type u_3
inst✝² : AddCommGroup V
inst✝¹ : Module ℚ V
inst✝ : FiniteDimensional ℚ V
φ : V ≃ₗ[ℚ] V
⊢ ∀ᶠ (v : IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum (RingOfIntegers ℚ)) in Filter.cofinite,
    Set.BijOn
      ⇑((fun v =>
            ContinuousLinearEquiv.toContinuousAddEquiv
              (@Aux.continuousBaseChange V inst✝² inst✝¹ inst✝ (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion ℚ v)
                (UniformSpace.Completion.commRing (WithVal (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.valuation ℚ v)))
                (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.instAlgebraAdicCompletion (RingOfIntegers ℚ) ℚ v)
                (UniformSpace.Completion.uniformSpace
                    (WithVal (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.valuation ℚ v))).toTopologicalSpace
                ⋯ φ))
          v)
      ↑((↑(@Aux.c_v_alg V inst✝² inst✝¹ v)).toAddMonoidHom.comp
            (LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
                (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ
                        v).subtype.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap).toAddMonoidHom).range
      ↑((↑(@Aux.c_v_alg V inst✝² inst✝¹ v)).toAddMonoidHom.comp
            (LinearMap.lTensor (IntegralLattice ℤ ℚ V)
                (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletionIntegers ℚ
                        v).subtype.toAddMonoidHom.toIntLinearMap).toAddMonoidHom).range

          with v _
        <;>
        (try simp) <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try
          {
            -- Prove that the map is bijective on the range
            have h₂ : Function.Bijective (φ : V → V) := φ.bijective
            -- Since φ is bijective, the induced map on the tensor product is also bijective
            have h₃ : Function.Bijective ((fun v ↦ ((Aux.continuousBaseChange
              (IsDedekindDomain.HeightOneSpectrum.adicCompletion ℚ v) φ).toContinuousAddEquiv)) v) := by
              -- The continuity and bijectivity are preserved under the tensor product
              simp_all [Aux.continuousBaseChange]
              <;>
              (try aesop)
              <;>
              (try
                {
                  -- Use the fact that φ is bijective to show that the tensor product map is bijective
                  have h₄ : Function.Bijective (φ : V → V) := φ.bijective
                  simp_all [LinearEquiv, LinearMap.Bijective]
                  <;>
                  (try aesop)
                })
            -- The map is bijective, hence a bijection on the range
            simp_all [Set.BijOn]
            <;>
            (try aesop)
          })
      exact h₁
  -- Use the fact that the map is bijective on the range to conclude that the scalar factor is 1
  simp_all [Filter.eventually_atTop]
  <;>
  (try aesop)
